race.name: Dartmoor Classic
race.tagline: Commit. Climb. Conquer. One of the UK's most prestigious open-road sportives.

climate.description: Dartmoor in June features mild temperate conditions typical of southwest England, with variable moorland weather including frequent wind and rain.

terrain.surface: Dramatic Dartmoor moorland with demanding ascents, exhilarating descents, and unrivalled scenery. All routes feature closed-road hill climb competition segments with iconic climbs including Haytor (featured in Tour of Britain).
features: Dramatic Dartmoor moorland with demanding ascents, exhilarating descents, and unrivalled scenery
All routes feature closed-road hill climb competition segments with iconic climbs including Haytor (featured in Tour of Britain)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 77. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 73.

course_description.character: Dramatic moorland challenge combining demanding ascents with exhilarating descents across Dartmoor's most iconic roads. The event showcases the rugged beauty of Dartmoor National Park with sweeping views and world-class event organization.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Grande route's 107-mile, 2,700m elevation gain challenge through Dartmoor's toughest climbs, culminating in the closed-road hill climb competition segment where riders compete for King/Queen of the Mountain honors.
suffering_zones: Haytor climb (featured in Tour of Britain) on Grande route
Multiple high-moorland ascents on Grande route delivering 2,700m total elevation gain
Closed-road hill climb competition segment on all routes (2026 feature)
Exposed moorland terrain with variable wind conditions typical of June in southwest England

logistics.lodging_strategy: Newton Abbot and surrounding Devon towns offer extensive accommodation options. Dartmoor is a popular tourist destination with hotels, B&Bs, and self-catering properties throughout the region.
logistics.transport: The event is based at Newton Abbot Racecourse with free parking and comprehensive support infrastructure. All routes are fully signposted with feed stations, mechanical assistance, and dedicated rider support teams. An event village hosts exhibitors, catering, entertainment, and family facilities.

history.origin_story: The Dartmoor Classic was established in 2007 by Mid-Devon Cycling Club as an annual sportive designed to bring a pro-tour cycling experience to the amateur cycling community. It has grown into one of the UK's most iconic and best-loved cycling events, regularly selling out with a field of 4,000 riders and earning recognition as one of Britain's premier open-road sportives.
notable_moments: Established 2007 as a grassroots initiative to challenge riders on Dartmoor's toughest terrain
Registered with British Cycling, the sport's governing body
Achieved sell-out status with 4,000-rider field, becoming a landmark fixture on the national cycling calendar
2026 expansion: closed-road hill climb competition segment added to all routes (previously limited to Grande route), with prizes for age/gender categories plus overall King/Queen of the Mountain
Consistently praised for exceptional volunteer organization, marshal support, and feed station quality
Attracts international participation, cementing status as one of the UK's most prestigious open-road sportives

biased_opinion.summary: The Dartmoor Classic represents the gold standard for UK sportive organization and course design. Established in 2007, it has earned its reputation as one of Britain's most prestigious open-road events through consistent delivery of exceptional experiences. The 2026 edition's expansion of the closed-road hill climb competition to all routes—not just the Grande—demonstrates the organizers' commitment to competitive engagement across all distances. For riders seeking a genuinely challenging moorland experience with world-class logistics and a welcoming community atmosphere, the Dartmoor Classic is essential.

final_verdict.one_liner: The Dartmoor Classic is the UK's premier moorland sportive, combining exceptional organization, iconic climbs, and genuine challenge in one of England's most dramatic landscapes.
final_verdict.should_you_race: The Dartmoor Classic stands as a landmark fixture on the national cycling calendar, delivering a pro-tour experience to amateur cyclists since 2007. With three distance options ranging from 45 to 107 miles, it accommodates diverse ability levels while maintaining uncompromising course quality. The Grande route's 2,700m elevation gain ensures legitimate suffering, while the closed-road hill climb competition on all routes adds prestige and competitive engagement. Mid-Devon Cycling Club's volunteer-driven organization is exemplary, with comprehensive support including mechanical assistance, professional medical coverage, and celebrated feed stations. The event's sell-out field of 4,000 riders and international reputation reflect its status as one of Britain's best-organized and most respected sportives. For serious cyclists seeking a challenging, well-executed event in stunning scenery, the Dartmoor Classic is non-negotiable.

videos.title: Dartmoor Classic | Devon’s premier road cycling sportive
videos.description:
The Dartmoor Classic Sportive offers some of Devon’s finest road cycling, leading riders high up onto the moor by a choice of beautiful routes.

Organised and run by the Mid-Devon Cycling Club, the event is open to 4000 cyclists, making it the largest cycling challenge in the South West of England. Established in 2007, the Dartmoor Classic quickly gained a reputation for being one of the best cycle sportives in the UK and attracts riders from around the world.

Featuring the hard working crew and volunteers that organise this amazing sportive, spectacular Dartmoor landscapes and thousands of keen cyclists, this is the 10th anniversary event held in June 2016. 

https://www.dartmoorclassic.co.uk

Filmed and edited by Carrie Hill
Soigneur/Production Assistant: Dave Henderson
Timelapse photography: Jon Crawford
Aerial footage: Lena Video with Skyflicks Media
Additional cameras: John Fitzsimons and Cineon Productions
Sound Mixer: Andrew Jarrett

Thank you for watching.

© Carrie Hill Films 2016

🚴🏻‍♂️ To see more cycling in Devon with one of the county’s legends you might like to watch https://youtu.be/O8h7kigZF-A

videos.title: Road Bikes - Devon Classic 2014
videos.description: Road Bikes - Kitsons Devon Classic 100 miles over Dartmoor from Exeter

terrain_notes.text: Muddy trails that catch riders off guard, with sections that can cause crashes - one rider had a massive fall just 20 minutes into the ride on the muddy terrain.

gear_mentions.text: Cross bike was mentioned as potentially being the wrong bike choice for the muddy Dartmoor conditions after a rider crashed on the terrain.

additional_quotes.text: It's like a big family - nobody gets left behind or forgotten. It's not literally just the 45 minutes you're racing, it's the whole day.

rider_intel.search_text: Riders describe Dartmoor's muddy trails as challenging and potentially treacherous, with crashes occurring early in rides due to the slippery conditions. The terrain features muddy trail sections that can catch cyclists off guard, particularly those on cross bikes which may not be optimal for the conditions. One rider experienced a significant fall just 20 minutes into their ride on the muddy terrain. The area offers scenic riding through Dartmoor National Park with a welcoming cycling community atmosphere. Riders emphasize the social aspect and family-like community feel of cycling events in the area, where the experience extends beyond just the riding itself to encompass the entire day's social interactions and camaraderie among participants.
